method_atom: A boundary-encoding (dimensional-reduction) principle in which the complete state of a bounded region of a higher-dimensional system is fully reconstructible from data stored on its lower-dimensional boundary, subject to a finite maximal information density of one discrete degree of freedom per fundamental area cell. This is combined with a complementary mechanism whereby elementary constituents grow in spatial extent as their momentum increases past a characteristic scale, with the spreading rate saturating the upper bound set by finite signal-propagation speed (causality).
object_atom: The three-dimensional gravitating world / spacetime whose consistent quantum description is sought at the Planck scale, together with high-energy particle collisions, information spreading near black-hole horizons, and its candidate realization in string theory (the light-front lattice string model).
brief_reason: The bounded-information-density boundary-encoding and the momentum-driven size-growth-saturating-causality machinery are the transferable methods; the Planck-scale quantum-gravity spacetime, particle collisions, black-hole horizons, and string-theory realization are the physics-specific objects.
